text: Level 5: Catastrophic Incident Injury involving member of the public or multiple worker deaths., Investigative/Reporting Requirements = → Immediate phone call to be scheduled with: o Local Team [ Identified on Page 1 of Emergency Action Plan ] o Regional Safety Manager [ Identified on Page 1 of Emergency Action Plan ] o Senior Director of Construction Safety: Shane Morrow o Executive Director of Construction: Tom Murphy o Senior Managing Director of Construction: Mike Gausden o GS Mountain Legal: Tim McDaniel o GS Mountain Risk Department: LaKecia Williams. Level 4: Fatality Single worker death, life-shortening health effect, or heath effect causing significant irreversible disability., Investigative/Reporting Requirements = → Immediate phone call to be scheduled with: o Local Team [ Identified on Page 1 of Emergency Action Plan ] o Regional Safety Manager [ Identified on Page 1 of Emergency Action Plan ] o Senior Director of Construction Safety: Shane Morrow o Executive Director of Construction: Tom Murphy o Senior Managing Director of Construction: Mike Gausden o GS Mountain Legal: Tim McDaniel o GS Mountain Risk Department: LaKecia Williams. Level 4: Potential A Near Miss or Recordable Injury that could've resulted in a 4 or a 5., Investigative/Reporting Requirements = → Incident must be treated similarly to a fatality. Regional Safety Manager must investigate incident, Lessons Learned must be identified and a Corrective Action Plan (CAP) must be put in place on the project. Worker Injury and Initial Incident Report and Fact Finding Form to be uploaded into Procore within 24 hours. Level 3: Recordable An injury or illness that caused unconsciousness, lost work days, restricted work activity, job transfers or medical care beyond first aid., Investigative/Reporting Requirements = → Investigation Requiring: o Project PM and Super o Crew involved in the incident o Incident Report to be uploaded into Procore within 24 hours → Worker Injury and Initial Investigation Report and Fact Finding Form Required. Level 2: First Aid Treatment given to someone injured, suddenly ill, etc., not requiring medical services, Investigative/Reporting Requirements = → Incident Report required, recommendations/or disciplinary actions posted in Procore within 24 hours → Worker Injury and Initial Investigation Report Required. Level 1: Near Miss An unplanned event that did not result in injury, illness, or damage but had the potential to do so, Investigative/Reporting Requirements = → Investigation required, recommendations/or disciplinary actions posted in Procore → Worker Injury and Initial Investigation Report Required
